ops.title: bridge OTR seeding + agent-browser connect gap
ops.body:
internal/bridge Seed now provisions an OFF-THE-RECORD CDP context so the decrypted session never persists to the on-disk --user-data-dir (closes the same-UID disk side-channel).

CDP call sequence (all browser-scope unless noted):
1. Target.createBrowserContext{disposeOnDetach:true} -> browserContextId
2. Target.createTarget{url:"about:blank", browserContextId} -> pageId (= the seeded page AND the client's attach target)
3. web storage (if any): attachToTarget{pageId,flatten} -> sessionId; Fetch.enable+Page.enable; per origin navigate + fulfill offline 200 + Runtime.callFunctionOn setter; navigate back to about:blank; Target.detachFromTarget (drops Fetch interception before handoff)
4. Storage.setCookies{cookies, browserContextId} (VERIFIED Chrome 150 accepts browserContextId; readback Storage.getCookies{browserContextId}) -- did NOT need the Network.setCookies fallback
5. Target.getTargets -> Target.closeTarget every page target != pageId (kills Chrome's default-context startup page)

Raw-CDP verify (bridge_test.go, -race, real Chrome): PASS. Client attaches to OTR page, navigates example.com offline, reads seeded cookie via document.cookie + Network.getCookies; OTR context has both cookies w/ correct attrs; default context has 0 cookies; whole-dir byte-scan + Default/Cookies SQLite (0 rows) prove nothing on disk.

CRITICAL agent-browser finding (0.31.1): `agent-browser connect <endpoint>` RELAUNCHES its own Chrome for a different cdp-url instead of driving the target -- verified identical for (a) plain --remote-debugging-port Chrome, (b) our default-context bridge, (c) our OTR bridge. So agent-browser's miss is NOT OTR-caused; reverting to default-context would NOT restore agent-browser and WOULD reopen the disk hole. The working attach verb is `agent-browser --cdp <port>` (drove plain Chrome). But the bridge is token-gated on the URL PATH, so --cdp's untokened GET /json/version is 403'd -> incompatible. The installed abwc skill 0.12.1 (bin/abwc-seed) does NOT CDP-connect to the bridge at all; it injects cookies into agent-browser's own browser.
